feed.title: SAP Community - FIN (Finance)
feed.subtitle: FIN (Finance) blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: El reto real del CFO actual
entry.content:
La mayoría de las organizaciones aún operan con procesos financieros que:

  • Dependen de reconciliaciones manuales
  • Tienen información fragmentada entre FI, CO y módulos satélite
  • Cierran tarde y con alta presión operativa
  • Reaccionan, en lugar de anticipar

El problema no es el equipo. Es la arquitectura financiera heredada.

SAP S/4HANA: Finanzas en tiempo real, no al cierre del mes

SAP S/4HANA redefine el rol de Finanzas a partir de un concepto clave: el Universal Journal.

Esto permite:

  • Un solo registro financiero (ACDOCA)
  • Eliminación de reconciliaciones entre FI y CO
  • Información consistente desde el origen
  • Reportes en tiempo real, sin esperar al cierre

Para el CFO, esto se traduce en algo muy concreto: confianza en los números, en cualquier momento.

Cierre financiero más rápido, con menos esfuerzo

Uno de los mayores dolores del CFO es el cierre mensual.

Con SAP S/4HANA:

  • Se automatizan tareas repetitivas
  • Se reducen ajustes de último minuto
  • El cierre deja de ser un evento traumático

El beneficio no es solo cerrar más rápido, sino liberar tiempo del equipo financiero para análisis y estrategia.

De control operativo a visión estratégica

SAP S/4HANA permite a Finanzas:

  • Analizar rentabilidad en tiempo real
  • Monitorear flujo de efectivo de forma predictiva
  • Detectar desviaciones antes de que se conviertan en problemas
  • Integrar analítica directamente en los procesos (Embedded Analytics)

Esto cambia la conversación del CFO con la Dirección General: de explicar resultados a guiar decisiones.

Automatización e inteligencia: menos operación, más valor

S/4HANA, combinado con soluciones como SAP BTP, IA y herramientas de automatización, habilita:

  • Conciliaciones automáticas
  • Cash Application inteligente
  • Detección de anomalías
  • Forecast financiero más preciso

No se trata de reemplazar personas, sino de elevar el rol del talento financiero.

Cloud, control y escalabilidad

Para muchos CFOs, la pregunta no es si migrar, sino cómo hacerlo sin perder control.

SAP S/4HANA ofrece:

  • Modelos Cloud y On-Premise
  • Seguridad y cumplimiento normativo
  • Escalabilidad para crecer sin rediseñar Finanzas cada pocos años

La clave es elegir el camino correcto alineado al modelo de negocio.

 

#CFO #SAP #S4HANA #FinanzasEstratégicas #TransformacionFinanciera #ERP #AI

entry.title: SAP Advanced Financial Closing: Third-Party Integration
entry.content:
Dear Finance Experts,

With the latest major release 6.0 of SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC) released on February 15, 2026, SAP introduces an important new capability: Third-Party System Integration.

This feature enables external systems to participate natively in AFC closing processes by acting as scheduling providers. It significantly expands AFC’s integration scope and allows customers to orchestrate financial close activities beyond SAP-managed execution engines.

Background

SAP Advanced Financial Closing has long been the strategic and central orchestration layer for financial close activities, coordinating tasks, dependencies, and monitoring across complex landscapes. With Third-Party System Integration, SAP formalizes this extensibility by introducing a public Scheduling Provider Interface. This allows external systems to:

  • Receive job execution requests from AFC

  • Report job execution status and results

  • Participate fully in AFC process monitoring and error handling

AFC remains the single point of orchestration, while execution can happen anywhere across the enterprise assuming that the Third-Party System Integration is properly enabled and set-up.

The first out-of-the-box integration using this approach is already available with SAP Accounting Automation by BlackLine.

Getting Started

At a high level, integrating a third-party system with AFC consists of three steps:

  1. Implement the AFC Scheduling Provider Interface
    Your system exposes REST endpoints that comply with the AFC scheduling contract.

  2. Register the provider in AFC
    The provider is configured in AFC as a communication system capable of executing tasks.

  3. Use AFC Closing Templates
    Closing tasks are modeled as usual in AFC, but assigned to the external provider.

SAP provides all necessary specifications and tooling via the SAP Business Accelerator Hub and GitHub.

Scheduling Provider Interface

The SAP Advanced Financial Closing Scheduling Provider Interface defines the protocol between AFC and an external communication system.

Key characteristics:

  • REST-based API

  • Clearly defined lifecycle operations

  • Status and result reporting back to AFC

Typical responsibilities of a third-party scheduling provider include:

  • Accepting task execution requests as jobs

  • Starting and monitoring jobs

  • Handling cancellations

  • Returning job results and messages

The complete API specification is available on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub:

  • SAP Advanced Financial Closing Scheduling Provider Interface

This specification is the definitive source and should be followed strictly to ensure compatibility with AFC.

SAP Advanced Financial Closing SDK for CDS

To simplify implementation, SAP provides the SAP Advanced Financial Closing SDK for CDS, published as an open-source project.

The Software Development Kit (SDK)

  • Is based on the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP)

  • Provides ready-to-use service definitions

  • Includes data models aligned with AFC semantics

  • Reduces boilerplate and integration effort

Using the SDK, developers can focus on:

  • Mapping AFC task executions to external job logic

  • Implementing business-specific processing

  • Handling system-specific scheduling and monitoring

The SDK architecture is summarized in the following diagram:

The SDK is available on GitHub and all the details can be found there:

  • SAP Advanced Financial Closing SDK for CDS

This makes it especially attractive for cloud-native integrations and rapid prototyping based on the SAP Business Technology Platform.

Hands-On

The following section shows in detail how to build a Third-Party Scheduling Provider implementing the Scheduling Provider Interface with the SAP Advanced Financial Closing SDK for CDS.


Prerequisites 

Setup Node.js: https://nodejs.org/en/download

Install CDS module globally

npm install -g @sap/cds


Step 1: Create AFC SDK based CAP Project

Start with a standard CAP Node.js project:
cds init scheduler --nodejs
cd scheduler

Add the AFC SDK dependency:

npm install @cap-js-community/sap-afc-sdk

Note: This blog leverages the CAP Node.js runtime. As the AFC SDK is also fully compatible with CAP Java runtime, third-party scheduling provider applications can also be based on a CAP Java projects, following the details in the SAP SDK landing page CAP Java section.


Step 2: Start Project

Start the generated project:

npm start

Open the application URL in browser: http://localhost:4004.

A welcome page is displayed giving an overview of the out-of-the-box functionality provided.

Specifically, the Scheduling Provider API implementing the Scheduling Provider Interface is served already at http://localhost:4004/api/job-scheduling/v1.

There is no content yet.

Step 3: Add Job Definitions

Install AFC SDK Command-Line-Interface (CLI)

npm install -g @cap-js-community/sap-afc-sdk

Add basic sample data for testing:

afc add sample -m

Project is extended to include CSV files with sample job definitions placed at /db/data.

Restart the generated project:

npm start

The Scheduling Provider API exposes automatically the job definitions at http://localhost:4004/api/job-scheduling/v1/JobDefinition (for example,JOB_1) and the corresponding parameters, for example, for  JOB_1 at http://localhost:4004/api/job-scheduling/v1/JobDefinition/JOB_1/parameters.

The CSV data can be adjusted according to the specific use-case. 

Step 4: Implement Job Logic

Add implementation stub to project:

afc add stub

Empty implementation files are placed at /srv:

  • srv/scheduling-processing-service.js: Hooks to implement custom logic for the job processing (create, update, cancel, sync, and so on) on processing level

  • srv/scheduling-provider-service.js: Hooks to implement custom logic for job creation and job cancellation on provider service level

Edit processJob hook in file srv/scheduling-processing-service.js to enhance the logic to automatically complete the job successfully after 5 seconds:

this.on(processJob, async (req, next) => {
  await this.triggerJobUpdate(req, req.job, JobStatus.completed, [{
    type: ResultType.message,
    name: "Message",
    messages: [{
      code: "jobCompleted",
      severity: MessageSeverity.info
    }],
  }], new Date(Date.now() + 5000)); // 5 seconds
  await next(); // set job status to running
});

Constants can be imported from AFC SDK as follows:

const {ResultType, MessageSeverity, JobStatus} = require("@cap-js-community/sap-afc-sdk");

The job processing logic can be adjusted according to the specific use-case.The idea is that the job processing is forwarded to the respective external job execution endpoint and that status updates are reported back asynchronously via this.processJobUpdate. Here this is simulated by a timer-based job update trigger.

Restart the generated project:

npm start

A new job instance now can be generated for, example, via curl:

curl -u alice:alice -X POST "http://localhost:4004/api/job-scheduling/v1/Job" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "JOB_1",
    "referenceID": "b001397c-b7f9-4f03-ba79-ec11297cae81",
    "parameters": [
      {
        "name": "A",
        "value": "abc"
      },
      {
        "name": "B",
        "value": 21
      },
      {
        "name": "C",
        "value": false
      }
    ]
  }'

The instance created returns the job ID with the initial status requested. Processing starts and shall update the job from status running to completed, when fetching the instance:

curl -u alice:alice -X GET "http://localhost:4004/api/job-scheduling/v1/Job/<jobID>"

With job status completed, the results of the job execution can be fetched via:

curl -u alice:alice -X GET "http://localhost:4004/api/job-scheduling/v1/Job/<jobID>/results"

Likewise result messages can be fetched via:

curl -u alice:alice -X GET "http://localhost:4004/api/job-scheduling/v1/JobResult/<jobResultID>/messages"

As alternative to curl, http files can be added (for example, at http/scheduling/provider.local.http)  to execute http requests against the local Scheduling Provider API, via command:

afc add http


Step 5: Add Security

The application must be secured before productive usage. Respective guidance can be found in CAP documentation: https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/security. Choose between XSUAA-based or IAS-based authentication and authorization to secure the APIs. In case of XSUAA, AFC SDK supports the creation of a Service Broker via command:

afc add broker​


Step 6: Deploy to Cloud

Project shall be prepared for deployment using the AFC SDK CLI:

Cloud Foundry: 

afc init cf

Kyma: 

afc init kyma

Additional guidance on cloud deployments can be found in CAP documentation: https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/deployment.

After successful deployment, the external scheduling provider is available via an internet-facing endpoint that can be reached by SAP Advanced Financial Closing through the SAP Destination Service.

Step 7: Create Destination

A destination to the external scheduling provider needs to be configured in the SAP BTP Cockpit on sub-account level in the Connectivity/Destinations section. The concrete destination configuration highly depends on the target application configuration.

An example destination configuration looks as follows (for example, OAuth2ClientCredentials)

destination:
  Name: "external-scheduler"
  tokenServiceURLType: "Dedicated"
  Type: "HTTP"
  clientId: "<client-id>"
  Authentication: "OAuth2ClientCredentials"
  clientSecret: "<client-secret>"
  tokenServiceURL: "https://authentication.sap.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token"
  ProxyType: "Internet"
  URL: "https://<url>/api/job-scheduling/v1"
  Description: "External Scheduler"

Fill the security-relevant configurations (clientId, clientSecret, tokenServiceURL, URL) from the client secret (IAS) or service key (XSUAA broker) from the specific setup of the scheduling provider application.

Step 8: Create Communication System

With the destination ready, a communication system of system type External System can be created in the SAP Advanced Financial Closing Specify Communication System app:


Step 9: Use External System in Template 

In the Manage Closing Task Lists app, you can include jobs from external communication system into the folder structure using the  Create Task from External Job button. A task list is generated as usual to process the external jobs.


Step 10: Process external jobs

External jobs integrated from external third-party systems can be processed as any other job-like task as part of the SAP Advanced Financial Closing scheduler and status and job results are synchronized afterwards:


With this implementation, the third-party system integration is fully demonstrated, proving that external jobs can be reliably processed within the automated scheduling framework of SAP Advanced Financial Closing.

Summary

SAP Advanced Financial Closing 6.0 introduces Third-Party System Integration, enabling external schedulers and execution engines to participate seamlessly in financial close processes. With the Scheduling Provider Interface and the AFC SDK, developers can quickly build compliant providers, manage job execution, and report statuses back to AFC. This new capability allows organizations to extend AFC beyond SAP-managed systems, integrate custom or cloud-native engines, and maintain centralized orchestration—all while reducing development complexity and ensuring reliable monitoring.

Thank you for taking the time and reading this blog post.

For more information on SAP S/4HANA Cloud, check out the following links:

  • SAP Community Page for SAP Advanced Financial Closing: here
  • SAP Help Portal for AFC: here
  • SAP Help Administration Guide - Third-Party Systems: here
  • SAP API Business Hub (SSPI): here
  • AFC in the SAP Roadmap Explorer: here
  • SAP.com page about AFC: here


Follow us via @SAP on twitter and tag #AdvancedFinancialClosing, or myself via @OliverKlemenz.

Disclaimer

Please note that SAP Advanced Financial Closing gets frequent updates and enhancements. Therefore, the current features and functions may not be exactly as described here. For the latest information, you can refer to the product documentation on the SAP Help Portal.

entry.title: Deciphering the Functional Mechanics of FYV.
entry.content:
Date: February 2026

Author:-  Er. Pratik Das (B.Tech, Mechanical Engineering , SAP Functional Consultant ).

Specialization :- MM, SCM Integration & Business Process Optimization.

Target Audience: SAP Functional Consultants (MM,FI), Solution Architects, Financial Controllers & Business Process Owners (BPO), SAP S/4HANA Migration Leads & etc.

In the SAP ecosystem, the Fiscal Year Variant (FYV) is far more than a simple calendar setting; it is the temporal foundation upon which all transactional integrity rests. For a Material Management (MM) Consultant, the FYV dictates the lifecycle of a Material Document, the calculation of moving average prices, and the strict boundaries of period-end closing via MMPV.

When we configure a system for a global rollout—balancing the Western (K4) and Indian (V3) statutory requirements—precision in OB29 is non-negotiable. Misconfiguration here doesn't just result in a ledger error; it can freeze a warehouse’s ability to post Goods Receipts (GR) or Issue Goods (GI).

  1. The Functional Mechanics of FYV :- 

The Fiscal Year Variant defines the number of posting periods in a fiscal year and the number of special periods. While the standard is 12 posting periods, SAP allows up to 16 periods in total.

  • Posting Periods: These correspond to the operational months where day-to-day procurement and inventory movements occur.
  • Special Periods (13–16): These are strictly for Finance (FI). As an MM consultant, it is vital to remember that logistics movements cannot be posted to special periods. These are reserved for audit adjustments, tax corrections, and year-end closing entries that do not affect physical inventory quantities.
  1. Calendar-Dependent vs. Year-Dependent: The Implementation Choice
  2. Calendar-Dependent (The "Set and Forget" Model)

Used predominantly in Western regions (Standard Variant K4), this configuration assumes the fiscal year is identical to the Gregorian calendar (January 1 to December 31).

  • Consultant Insight: When "Calendar Year" is flagged in OB29, the system ignores the "Period" and "Day" sub-tables. It automatically knows January has 31 days and is Period 01. This is the lowest-maintenance path for a Greenfield implementation.
  1. Year-Dependent (The High-Precision Model)

A variant is "Year-Dependent" if the start and end dates of the periods change every year.

  • The 4-4-5 Retail Cycle: Common in US/UK retail, where months are defined as cycles of weeks to ensure every quarter has exactly 13 weeks. Here, "Month 1" might end on January 28th one year and January 29th the next.
  • Configuration Necessity: Every year, the consultant or the power user must manually maintain the period end-dates for the upcoming year. Failure to do this before the clock strikes midnight on New Year's Eve will result in a global "Period not defined" hard error for every Purchase Order (PO) and GR.
  1. Geographical Logic: Western (K4) vs. Indian (V3)

The primary differentiator in global rollouts is the Year Shift. This is a displacement factor ($+1$, $0$, or $-1$) that aligns the calendar year with the financial year.

The Western Style (K4)

The fiscal year matches the calendar year.

  • January: Period 01 / Shift 0
  • December: Period 12 / Shift 0

The Indian Style (V3)

The Indian fiscal year starts in April. This creates a technical overlap that requires a displacement shift.

  • April to December: These months are part of the current calendar year. Year Shift = 0.
  • January to March: These months belong to the next calendar year but the current fiscal year. To bring them back into the current fiscal year's reporting, we apply a Year Shift = -1.

Consultant Trap: If you are configuring a new Indian Company Code and forget the $-1$ shift for Jan/Feb/March, the system will try to post a March 2026 invoice into Fiscal Year 2026 instead of 2025, throwing the entire Balance Sheet out of equilibrium.

  1. Case Study: Shortened Fiscal Year During Corporate Realignment

The Scenario:

An Indian subsidiary of a German conglomerate is migrating its legacy systems to the global SAP S/4HANA template. The Indian entity currently follows V3 (April–March), but the German parent company mandates K4 (January–December) for global consolidation starting January 1st, 2026.

The Solution: The "Transition Variant"

You cannot simply change the variant in OB37 mid-year; doing so would corrupt the Material Ledger history.

  1. Define the Shortened Year: In OB29, create a new Year-Dependent variant (e.g., Z9).
  2. Period Compression: For the transition year (2025), you define only 9 periods (April 2025 to December 2025).
  3. The Cutover: On December 31st, 2025, the "Year 2025" ends.
  4. The Re-assignment: In the new year, the Company Code is assigned to the standard K4 variant.

Critical MM Checklist for this Transition:

  • MMPV: Ensure the last period of the shortened year (Period 09) is closed correctly.
  • Purchase Orders: Any open POs with delivery dates in the "missing" months (Jan-March) must be reviewed to ensure the new period logic doesn't block the GR.
  • Physical Inventory: A full wall-to-wall count is recommended at the end of the shortened year to "cleanse" the valuation before the new cycle begins.
  1. Integration Points for the MM Consultant

While FI defines the variant, the MM consultant must manage the operational impact:

  • T-Code OMSY: This is where we see the link between the Company Code and the Fiscal Year. It shows the current, previous, and last period of the previous year.
  • T-Code MMPV: This is the "Period Closer." It checks against the FYV to move the "Current Period" forward. If your FYV says Period 12 ends on March 31, but you try to run MMPV on March 30, the system will prevent the shift.
  • Material Valuation: SAP stores material stocks and values by period. If the FYV is changed without a proper migration strategy, the historical stock tables (MARCH, MARDH) will become inconsistent, leading to severe audit failures.

Conclusion

The Fiscal Year Variant is the heartbeat of SAP. Whether you are managing a standard K4 setup or a complex Year-Dependent shortened year for a merger, the logic remains the same: align the calendar to the business cycle through the Year Shift.

entry.title: SAP Finance Certifications: Hands-On Testing in Live SAP S/4HANA Systems
entry.content:
Why SAP Finance Certifications Matter

SAP Finance certifications demonstrate your ability to not just understand concepts, but to actually execute them in a working system. Whether you're a financial or management accounting implementation consultant, these certifications prove you can deliver results from day one.

Financial Accounting Certifications

1. SAP Certified - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Financial Accounting

Learn More & Register

This certification validates your fundamental knowledge and practical skills in financial accounting processes within SAP S/4HANA. During the exam, you'll work directly in a live SAP S/4HANA system to demonstrate your ability to:

  • Process accounts payable and receivable transactions
  • Handle general ledger accounting
  • Manage asset accounting
  • Execute period-end closing activities

2. SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Financial Accounting

Learn More & Register

Focused on cloud implementations, this certification proves your consulting capabilities in deploying SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for financial accounting. The practical exam format ensures you can configure and implement solutions in real-world cloud scenarios.

Management Accounting Certifications

3. SAP Certified - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Management Accounting

Learn More & Register

This certification covers the controlling and management accounting aspects of SAP S/4HANA. Through hands-on exercises in a live system, you'll demonstrate proficiency in:

  • Cost center accounting
  • Internal orders
  • Product costing
  • Profitability analysis
  • Profit center accounting

4. SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Management Accounting

Learn More & Register

This certification prepares you to implement management accounting solutions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition environments, with practical testing that mirrors real implementation projects.

The Practical Exam Advantage

What makes these certifications truly valuable is the hands-on exam methodology. Instead of memorizing answers, you'll:

  • Work in a live SAP S/4HANA system during your exam
  • Perform actual transactions and configurations that reflect real business scenarios
  • Demonstrate problem-solving skills in a realistic environment
  • Prove you can deliver from your first day on the job

This practical approach means that when you earn your certification, employers know you have genuine, applicable skills.

Which Certification Is Right for You?

  • Choose Financial Accounting certifications if you work with general ledger, accounts payable/receivable, asset accounting, or financial reporting
  • Choose Management Accounting certifications if you focus on cost controlling, internal reporting, profitability analysis, or management decision support
  • Choose on-premise certifications for traditional SAP S/4HANA implementations
  • Choose Cloud Public Edition certifications if you're working with or transitioning to cloud-based solutions

Ready to Get Certified?

SAP Finance certifications are investments in your career that pay dividends through enhanced credibility, expanded opportunities, and demonstrated expertise. With the practical exam format, you'll gain confidence knowing that your certification reflects real-world capabilities.

Visit the links above to explore the detailed exam guides, recommended training courses, and registration information for each certification path. Your journey to becoming an SAP Certified Finance professional starts with hands-on experience and that's exactly what these certifications test!

Have you taken any of these SAP Finance certifications? Share your experience below!

entry.title: Mastering Purchase Order Accruals in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
The SAP S/4HANA Accrual Engine, Accruals Accounting offers flexible configuration options like,

  • Calculation and posting of the accruals
  • Option to review process for the monthly accrual amounts
  • Supports monitoring and the approval of accruals

POAC Introduction

The Purchase Order Accruals application enables you to calculate and post accruals in General Ledger Accounting automatically.

The relevant data is transferred from the Materials Management component to the accrual engine and automatically converted from purchase order items into accrual items. The system calculates the amount to accrue for each purchase order item based on the above data. You can also simulate these amounts.

The functions in Purchase Order Accruals support parallel Accounting. This means that you can calculate and post accruals simultaneously according to different ledger groups. You can start an accrual run, which posts all accruals for the various business transactions.

From a technical standpoint, the Purchase Order Accruals application is built on the accrual engine. The purchase order items to be accrued are managed in Purchase Order Accruals. The processes for calculating and posting accruals, on the other hand, are managed in the Accrual Engine.

To post-purchase order accruals, perform the following steps:

  • In the MM component, create a purchase order
  • The purchase order is then transferred to the Accrual Engine offline or online, and monthly accrual amounts are calculated and displayed in the Accrual Engine
  • (Optional) Review and approve, or just approve the monthly accrual amounts
  • Post the accrual amounts.


Accruals Process
The purchase order accruals process involves the following job templates and apps in Fiori:

  • The Propose Periodic Accruals job template in the Schedule Accruals Jobs app
  • Review Purchase Order Accruals - My Inbox or Review Purchase Order Accruals - for Cost Accountant
  • The Post Periodic Accruals job template in the Schedule Accruals Jobs app
  • Analyse Accruals Posting
  • Display Purchase Order Accruals

Integration
In Financial Accounting, you can also post accruals with the recurring entries function. In contrast to recurring entries, the functions in Purchase Order Accruals offer you the following benefits:

  • Automatic calculation based on live PO data.
  • Pre-posting review/approval.
  • Complex rules (e.g., pro-rata, thresholds).

Purchase Order Accruals: Design

 

Purchase Order Accruals Design

POAC Steps
Creation of a purchase order in application component Purchasing MM-PUR, for example using transaction “Create Purchase Order” (ME21N).

Each purchase order is automatically transferred into the Accrual Engine. This happens by Customizing settings and controlling whether and how accruals will be posted. In the Accrual Engine, an accrual object is created for each purchase order.

While implementing the application component Purchase Order Accruals, you have to transfer your existing purchase orders once into the Accrual Engine of your production system. You can do these using accrual activities “Transfer Purchase Orders to Accrual Engine” with schedule accruals jobs app ID F3778.

Display Accrual Objects:
For each purchase order, you can display the monthly accrual amounts the system calculates using app “Display Accrual Objects items” app ID F4899.

Schedule Accruals Jobs:
This app enables you to schedule accruals jobs using the templates provided. For example, you can perform periodic posting runs and propose periodic accrual amounts. 

You can use the scheduling options to carry out the following periodic accrual activities:

  • Perform Periodic Posting Runs
  • Propose Period Amounts
  • Transfer Purchase Orders to Accrual Engine

The templates are available only when you've registered them in customizing view APJ_C_SCOPE . Follow the steps to register for the three templates:

  1. Run t-code SM30.
  2. Enter APJ_C_SCOPE for Table/View, and click Maintain.
  3. Add the following new entries:
    • SAP_FIN_GL_ACE_POSTING_RUN
    • SAP_FIN_GL_ACE_PROPOSAL_RUN
    • SAP_FIN_GL_POAC_MMPO2ACE_TRANS
  4. Save your changes.

 

Schedule Accruals Jobs

 

Template Selection

Optional: Review and approval process at the end of each fiscal period:

  • At the end of each fiscal period, a reviewer can adjust the accrual amounts (or planned costs) that the system proposes for the current fiscal period.
  • As a prerequisite, the proposal amounts must be generated for the given fiscal period using Accrual Engine for POs - Propose Period Amounts under schedule accruals jobs.
  • The review can be performed using the app “Review Purchase Order Accruals for Cost Accountant” app ID F3552. The review includes the possibility to manually adjust the accrual amounts that the system proposes.
  • As an alternative or in addition to the review, approval can be performed using the transaction “Approve Periodic Accruals”.
  • The difference between review and approval is that in case only review is active, then the system will post the proposed accrual amounts even if no review was done. But if approval is active, then only those accrual amounts are posted which have been approved. 

Post accruals at the end of each fiscal period: 

The accrual amounts are posted using accrual activities “Periodic Posting Run” with schedule accruals jobs app ID F3778.

Analyze Accrual Postings: 

With this application you can display an overview of accrual postings, you can filter the postings by various criteria and navigate to a specific posting in related apps to investigate. (App ID F3732)

Display Accrual Objects

  • An accrual object contains all data that the system needs to calculate and post accruals for this business transaction.
  • Optional: Reviewing and Approving Periodic Accruals
  • You can set up a review and approval procedure for the accrual amounts calculated and proposed by the system.

Customizing Purchase Order Accruals

  • Before you can use the functions in Purchase Order Accruals, you must make extensive Customizing settings.

  • The settings control, for example, the purchase orders or purchase order items to be accrued, the algorithms to be used for a particular purchase order item, and the accounts to be used for accruals posting.
  • If you want to take the optional step of manually checking and approving accrual amounts, you must also make the corresponding Customizing settings here.
  • Additionally, some settings are available to you with which you can adjust the functions according to your needs. For example, you can add user-defined fields (or parameters) for use in the functions. 

Posting Data of an Accrual Object

For each accrual object, the item data is saved in the journal.

Prerequisites:

You have made the necessary settings in Customizing for accrual posting, that is, you have, for example, defined accrual item categories, posting schemas, and account determination. You find these settings in Customizing for Accrual Management.

Closing Notes:

Details of the Purchase order Accruals process can be obtained under Scope item 2VB in SAP Best Practices Explorer. POAC streamlines month-end but PO lifecycle changes (e.g., changes, cancellations) impact accruals—test thoroughly in production-like scenarios.

Thanks for reading! Feedback welcome to refine future posts.

entry.title: SAP and BlackLine: Enhancing the Gold Standard Partnership with AI Capabilities
entry.content:
As the use of AI becomes common practice among finance and accounting teams, SAP and BlackLine are reviewing capabilities to reduce the time and effort our customers spend on financial closing and record-to-report (R2R) activities, all while upholding the highest standards of integrity and compliance.

Building on the gold standard our partnership has set for record-to-report (R2R), we are now exploring how  the capabilities of SAP Joule and BlackLine Verity AI can work together to enhance the gold standard for our joint customers.

While SAP has tens of thousands of partners, only a few dozen have reached the status of solution extension. At this partnership level, SAP and BlackLine product teams review select and relevant development plans and product roadmaps to ensure alignment and interoperability.  When companies choose SAP and BlackLine to support their mission critical financial close and record-to-report activities, they are choosing the gold standard.

This collaboration can unlock greater efficiency and productivity enabling customers to achieve more. By aligning technologies and expertise, both SAP and BlackLine are committed to delivering enhanced AI products that work better together to deliver value and help drive transformative outcomes for finance teams worldwide. We look forward to providing further updates as our joint work on AI progresses.

Learn more about SAP solutions for accounting automation here:

https://www.sap.com/products/financial-management/account-substantiation-automation-blackline.html

entry.title: What’s new for FINANCE TEAMS in the February 2026 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition?
entry.content:
This week, SAP released SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 FPS1.

While there are more than 750 incremental enhancements available in the What’s New Viewer your team can dig into, there are a couple that are game-changing for your finance teams. This blog goes through all of the areas of Finance to highlight the innovations that are most relevant for finance.

 

Financial Planning and Analysis – more support for complex allocations

In the area of management accounting, cost controllers have more automation than ever before with SAP S/4HANA’s universal allocation capabilities. Controllers run allocation cycles to move overhead costs, revenues, and quantities across cost centers, profit centers, and market segments. This release introduces a new Fiori app called Manage Allocation Changes (Fiori app ID F7272) to improve the traceability of changes made to allocation cycles. This record of the cycle definition used for each live run is important to answer questions about allocations.

Accounting and Financial Close – SAP Business AI for automation and usability

In financial accounting, most journal entries are automatically created by logistical processes. However, for those manual journals that originate in finance, this release includes an AI capability to automate many journal entry types - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, journal upload. By training the AI capability with accounting policy or other guidance documents, the AI-generated journal posting proposals meet highest quality standards. The accountants approve the proposal, and the proposals are posted. Thousands of journal lines otherwise calculated in spreadsheets are processed automatically – in line with SAP data structures, SAP business data validation rules, and SAP Business AI principles.

SAP Business AI is also used in the depreciation key explanation feature that uses natural language to explain to business users how depreciation keys operate within asset accounting. With a clearer understanding of the underlying logic, asset accountants can ensure values are being calculated accurately – improving the quality of financial statements. Accounting is a key area in Finance that is undergoing a “generational change” – and new accountants will benefit from these explanations.

In the area of the entity close, SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC Community Site) has released several features that help large and complex organizations add even more automation capabilities to their financial close:

Improved automation for complex landscapes:

  • Third-party system integration – execute tasks in external systems while keeping central visibility across all systems involved in the customers' closing process, including integration with SAP Accounting Automation by BlackLine.

Faster setup for project teams:

  • Mass task creation via spreadsheet – accelerate your entity close optimization projects during the initial setup of the task list template

Improved compliance and control of the close:

  • Simplify how you push out central changes in your closing templates to all entities – and how you can standardize reporting attributes across templates – with improved task group maintenance functionality
  • Tailor your reporting based on the way you structure your business with flexible organizational groupings(company code, controlling areas, and plants). Dedicated views make it easy to identify overdue tasks and monitoring closing progress across units. 
  • Let your closing process owners determine the settings when comments are required to ensure compliance requirements are met - without unnecessary overhead in non-production systems.

In the area of SAP Central Finance, which extends the value of your SAP S/4HANA finance processes across your landscape, even to parts of your organization that operate in separate ERP systems, this release includes:

Improved visibility and reporting with centralized GL postings:

  • Enables Finance users to manage their G/L postings in a central place with detailed and summary level financial reporting and analytics
  • Leverages the Universal Journal, allowing organizations to report on various dimensions of their business as a self-service
  • Allows users to make decisions quickly and identify bottlenecks and pain points that need to be corrected

Better working capital visibility with Taulia in your central finance landscape:

  • Offers tools to optimize working capital, including supply chain finance, dynamic discounting, and virtual cards
  • Provides seamless integration to central finance, providing a consolidated view of working capital across the company
  • Improves cash flow while protecting supplier relationships
  • Helps organizations improve liquidity and strengthen supply chain resilience

In the area of revenue accounting there are several new capabilities, including business reconciliation features to integrate results analysis with optimized contract management and inbound processing. There’s more flexibility for contract combinations and performance obligation reassignments, as well as support for the early termination of performance obligations.

There are also new innovations in event-based revenue recognition, including the “revenue-based percentage of completion” method for services with advanced execution, as well as revenue recognition for execution order items with resource-related cost items. There is also new support for event-based revenue recognition for sales orders with milestone billing.

For the full set of details on these innovation, there’s a replay session available.

Treasury and Working Capital  - agentic automation for operational excellence

In Contract Accounting, this release includes an advanced AI feature - a Dispute Agent (video available at link!) that helps accountants resolve dispute cases related to incorrect invoices or missing payments efficiently. The agent ensures timely collections and protects customer relationships by proactively proposing and executing next steps – such as creating a credit memo to resolve the conflict. You can register for beta testing here.

In the area of Cash and Treasury, this release includes a Cash Management Agent that helps cash managers save time by automating reconciliations and increasing interest yields by proposing cash optimizations.

Studies have shown great results:

70% Reduction in cash management effort

You can register for beta testing here: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3987

In SAP Treasury and Risk Management, SAP has delivered support for an alternative risk-free currency for Balance Sheet FX Risk. Hedge Management’s Balance Sheet FX Risk process provides a snapshot of exposures that are in a currency that is different from the risk-free currency – which by default is the currency of the company code. However, for companies operating in countries where it is difficult to obtain hedging transactions in the risk-free company code currency, or in countries where the company code currency is not stable, it may be necessary to use an alternative risk-free currency – and this is now possible as part of the standard process.

Innovations beyond Finance

Moving beyond “core finance”, there are some innovations, like new automation support for complex multi-stage intercompany processes, that will make lives easier for Finance teams. Any time new standard processes are introduced, with more automation and fewer possibilities for error, your team benefits. You will deal with fewer manual adjustments at period-end, fewer spreadsheets, and fewer reconciliations. This new process support has been delivered in restricted release to help manage multistage intercompany sales and stock transfers with flexibility, optimizing supply chain efficiency. Additionally, Universal Parallel Accounting simplifies financial management by managing parallel value and currency flows, supporting compliance with IFRS and local GAAP. These capabilities provide comprehensive insights and operational flexibility, ensuring seamless coordination and cost optimization across your business. More background can be found here.

Finally, there's a Global Webinar planned for Thursday, March 12 as a part of the Continuous Innovation for SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud series. Don't miss this opportunity to learn more about how to get the most of your SAP solutions.

entry.title: Introducing SAP Accounting Automation by BlackLine
entry.content:
Recently SAP announced the availability of SAP Accounting Automation by BlackLine.  This is a very important expansion of the SAP Solution Extension Portfolio from BlackLine which all SAP by BlackLine customers can begin to utilize right away.  This article introduces and summarizes the key features and benefits of the new SAP Accounting Automation by BlackLine solution. 

First, introducing SAP Accounting Automation by BlackLine provides enhanced capabilities for the SAP + BlackLine gold standard for record to report as noted below.   Also with this announcement, BlackLine is rebranding its solution extensions by BlackLine portfolio from "SAP financial close solutions by BlackLine" to "SAP accounting automation solutions by BlackLine” by using a name that better reflects the end-to-end automation capabilities that the solutions provide.

 

Second, SAP Accounting Automation by BlackLine is designed to bring comprehensive line of sight to the enterprise financial close by improving visibility and automation through unified accounting processes.  SAP Accounting Automation by Blackline leverages specific capabilities of BlackLine Studio360 to make this happen.  So for the first time, joint SAP and solution extensions by BlackLine customers can now integrate the available BlackLine process templates and the status of BlackLine sub-processes right from within SAP Advanced Financial Closing during the financial close by adding a BlackLine Process template to a SAP Advanced Financial Closing task as shown below.  This can further improve efficiency and create capacity across the financial close.

 

The first version of SAP Accounting Automation by BlackLine is focused on orchestration of BlackLine sub-processes and integration with SAP Advanced Financial Closing and has successfully completed SAP’s Premium Qualification process with an official launch date of Feb 16, 2026.   An example of a BlackLine process template showing BlackLine sub-processes which can now be included in a closing task for SAP Advanced Financial Closing is shown below.  End users can also create and modify additional process templates establishing a library of process templates helpful for continuous improvement activities related to the financial close each month. 

 

An example BlackLine process template showing BlackLine sub-processes which can now be included in a closing task for SAP Advanced Financial Closing.

 

 

 

The new integration component within SAP Advanced Financial Closing enables the connection to SAP Accounting Automation by BlackLine and is available by default for SAP Advanced Financial Closing customers on version 6.0 available Feb 16th 2026.

SAP Accounting Automation by BlackLine is currently a no-charge entitlement to existing and new solution extensions by BlackLine (SolEx) customers.  The current product scope of "SAP Accounting Automation by BlackLine“ is orchestration of BlackLine sub-processes and integration with SAP Advanced Financial Closing.

Existing solution extensions by BlackLine (SolEx) customers should submit a customer support ticket to SAP to request activation of "SAP Accounting Automation by BlackLine“.   Future capabilities of BlackLine Studio360 will be evaluated for inclusion in SAP Accounting Automation by BlackLine.   

So in conclusion, SAP Accounting Automation by BlackLine is a very important expansion to solution extensions by BlackLine portfolio which all solution extensions by BlackLine customers can begin to utilize right away.   A key example is that BlackLine process templates and the status of BlackLine sub-processes can be viewed directly from within SAP Advanced Financial Closing which can further improve efficiency and create capacity across the financial close.

For more information on SAP Accounting Automation by BlackLine, please listen to the webinar or visit the following helpful links: 

short flyover video     long flyover video     video-blog     solution brief  SAP Help Portal

third party integration

Also see https://www.blackline.com/about/press-releases/ .

entry.title: Using SAP Advanced Financial Closing to Schedule Jobs For Financial Consolidation
entry.content:
This blog briefly outlines how customers can use the SAP Advanced Financial Closing solution to schedule jobs when performing financial consolidations.  For SAP Cloud ERP customers, this typically means also utilizing the group reporting consolidations functionality.

SAP Advanced Financial Closing is SAP’s strategic cloud-based orchestration and intelligent automation hub for record to report, allowing customers to automate and shorten the legal entity close cycle times while also offering superb planning, automation and monitoring for the financial close. 

One question often asked by customers that came up again during the recent February release highlights webinar was can SAP Advanced Financial Closing be used to automate jobs when performing consolidations?

The answer is yes for specific scenarios as outlined in the SAP note 3716576 or in the help documentation. For example, SAP SE is using both SAP Advanced Financial Closing and group reporting and is managing several group reporting jobs from within SAP Advanced Financial Closing during the quarter end close. 

SAP SE is using standard SAP Advanced Financial Closing functionality where a job task type executes the group reporting programs with parameters sourced from both the task model template (static/variant configuration) and the Header (dynamic values like fiscal year and period).

The integration is implemented through a task list model containing three group reporting tasks, each configured with a separate task model (Data Monitor tasks: Release Universal Journal, Calculation of Retained Earnings, Currency Translation), where parameter substitution automatically passes runtime values from the close template into the backend job variants during execution.

With a small amount of backend system set-up, you can leverage SAP Advanced Financial Closing’s built-in orchestration and parameter mapping capabilities to coordinate multiple consolidation processes within a single financial close workflow. 

The jobs Universal Journal Release: FINCS_DATA_RELEASE_TASK_JOB, Retained Earnings Calculation: FINCS_ANI_ANI00, and Currency Translation: FINCS_CTR_CTR00 are run in defined intervals. Executions follow a strict timetable with authorized users assigned. This setup ensures timely and consistent results, reduces manual effort, and safeguards data quality for follow-on processes. The solution supports repeat runs and is fully embedded in SAP Advanced Financial Closing.

So, in conclusion, SAP SE is another example of how customers are using SAP Advanced Financial Closing to automate different recurring tasks within their accounting and reporting function.  Above, we have shown examples of using SAP Advanced Financial Closing to manage jobs within SAP Cloud ERP, group reporting to reduce manual effort and improve accuracy of the financial close.  For more details, please consult note 3716576 or the help documentation.

entry.title: Direct Debit Mandates with SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity
entry.content:
This blog post explains how SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity (MBC) is used in the direct debit mandate procedures in Contract Accounting in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

You can find more information about the following scenarios with the banks:

  1. Sending Outbound Messages of Mandate Changes:
    When you want to create direct debit mandates or make any mandate changes, you send messages to the bank with the MBC.
  2. Receiving Inbound Messages from Banks:
    After the bank receives your message, you get the messages, such as notification of mandate changes, rejected direct debit instructions, or the cancelled direct debit payments, from the bank through MBC.

You can find more information about the MBC in this blog post Multi Bank Connectivity Overview.

You can learn more about direct debit mandates in the following blog posts

  • How to authorize Direct Debit Payments outside SEPA in Contract Accounting
  • Bank Communication Using Direct Debit Mandate in SAP S4/HANA Public Cloud
  • Notification of Direct Debit Mandate Changes from Banks
  • Synchronization for Direct Debit Mandates
  • Direct Debit Advance Notices

Scenario 1: Outbound Messages (Blue Part of the Diagram)

You send messages relevant to mandate changes or mandate creation to the bank using MBC connection. In this procedure, the messages are sent out to the bank automatically,  especially for more messages that are sent in batches at once, informing about the mandate creation or changes. The involvement of the MBC improves the efficiency.

Prerequisites

 You have maintained the routing settings in the Maintain Routing Settings for Outbound Messages activity (ID: 103501) as follows:

Sender IDReceiver ID Message Type Origin

< SUN number>

Service User Number		GB_BACSGB_AUDDISFICA

These message types are used for the MBC to identify the messages that are sent to the banks (receiver).
Note: You can use the Cloud BAdI BADI_DDM_BKINST_MBC_OUT_PARAMS to change Sender Id, Receiver ID, and Message Type. These values are used in this Maintain Routing Settings for Outbound Messages activity.

Procedure

  1. You have created a direct debit mandate or a made a mandate change.
  2. Open the Create Direct Debit Mandate Instructions Media app (F7520). When you schedule a run, select the Send Message (MBC) checkbox.
  3. To see the processed messages, go to the Manage Bank Messages app (F4385).
    Note: Ensure that you have the same minimum authorization as delivered with the Business Integration Specialist - Bank integration (SAP_BR_BANK_INT_SPECIALIST) business role template.
    Select the message types that you have configured.

    See the messages that have been sent to the bank.
  4. You can view or download the files in the Manage Direct Debit Mandate Media app.

Scenario 2: Inbound Messages (Red Part of the Diagram)

After you sent the outbound messages to the bank, you may receive messages back as follows:

  • Notification of mandate changes if the mandate changes are applied
  • Rejected direct debit instructions if the mandate changes are rejected

After you execute the payments with direct debit, you may receive from the bank messages about cancelled direct debit payments.

Prerequisites

  1. You have created a direct debit mandate or a made a mandate change.
  2. You have maintained the routing settings in the Maintain Processing Steps for Inbound Messages activity (ID: 103504) as follows:

    Sender ID

    Receiver ID

    Message Type

    Application

    <House bank ID>

    <Your ID>

    GB_ADDACS

    DDM_NOC

    <House bank ID>

    <Your ID>

    GB_AUDDIS_REJ

    DDM_NOC

    <House bank ID>

    <Your ID>

    GB_ARUDD

    DDM_RJCT


    Note: Messages delivered through the MBC carries the following types of files:

    • Changes to direct debit mandates or rejections to mandate changes (application type DDM_NOC)
    • Cancelled direct debit payments (application type DDM_RJCT)

    Upon receiving the messages from the banks through MBC, the messages are categorized based on the application type and proceed with different actions accordingly.

    • For the files that are assigned with the DDM_NOC application type, the system automatically imports the files in the Import Direct Debit Mandate Instructions Media app.
    • For the files that are assigned with the DDM_RJCT application type, the system automatically creates jobs in the Manage Incoming Payment Files app.

In the following diagram, you can see how the MBC automatically processes files from the banks based on the application types. After the automatic processing, you can view the processed messages in the Manage Bank Messages app.
Note: To access this app, you have the same minimum authorization as delivered with the Business Integration Specialist - Bank Integration (SAP_BR_BANK_INT_SPECIALIST) business role template.

3. You have maintained the statement parameter set assignments in the Maintain Processing Steps for Inbound Messages (ID: 103504) activity.

Optionally, you can implement your own logic by using the Business Add-In (BAdI) BADI_DDM_NOC_MBC_IMPORT_PARAMS to identify the messages sent by banks and convert them into the Import Direct Debit Mandate Instructions Media app.

Procedure

You can directly use the Manage Bank Messages app (F4385) to view messages as follows:

Select the message you’d like to view.

Select the file to download and view the XML file.

Is this blog post useful to you? Leave your comments and let us know your thoughts?

entry.title: Verify General Journal Entries Flexible Workflow, and Extensibility
entry.content:
Journal Entry Upload Requirements

Do you have a custom solution for uploading journal entries with workflow approvals and email notifications? Are you converting from ECC to S/4HANA and would like to bring your custom code back to clean core? Is Fiori part of UX Journey? If you answered yes to any of those, then this blog post is for you.

Custom Code Solutions

Before the advent of Fiori apps, SAP customers had to create their own custom code to manage the uploading of journal entries via Excel. I have worked with customers that have different solution design when it comes to journal upload. They come in various shapes and sizes like LSMW, custom programs, and even third-party tools. Those were the days when functional consultants had to be creative and work together with technical consultants to address the business requirements around journal entries upload and approvals.

Fiori Apps and Extensibility towards Clean Core

In SAP S/4HANA, we now have a set of standard Fiori Apps that can address the same business requirements and maintain a clean core. This blog post (based on SAP S/4HANA 2023) is a result of different customer projects with varying business requirements. It ranges from a simple (greenfield) to highly complex (system conversion) custom built solutions. 

The information presented intends to equip the functional and technical consultants with Fiori Apps and available extensibility when it comes to journal entries. Here is the list of Fiori Apps that needs to be activated and deployed depending on business requirements.

Fiori App IDFiori App NameDescription Tips and Tricks
F2547AVerify General Journal Entries - For RequesterAs a requester (for example, an accountant), with the Verify General Journal Entries - For Requester app, you can create, display and edit a journal entry, and then submit it for verification. In addition, you could upload the general journal entries via EXCEL or CSV file and then submit multiple journal entries for verification. For the rejected ones, you could resubmit for verification. Afterwards, you can track status and whole change history for workflow.This app can be used as the single point of entry for users to upload a journal entry.
F2548

Upload General Journal Entries

With this app, you can upload multiple general journal entries from a spreadsheet or a CSV file. The template for uploading is available in this app. After you successfully finish the upload, you can post the general journal entries directly. Postings can be made in all relevant ledgers for the accounts for which you are responsible. If not all the journal entries in a file are uploaded successfully, you must correct errors, copy the batch ID of the initial upload to the file, and then repeat upload.

You can navigate to this Fiori App from F2547A via the Upload button.

If you need to enforce that attachments should be mandatory, you can hide the Submit button and users have to go inside the uploaded journal entry to upload attachments. The Submit button will only be clicked after attaching the supporting documentation. An error message could be raised if the attachment is not greater than zero.

F2720

Manage Workflows for Journal Entry Verification - In General Ledger

Before you start to verify general journal entries or currency adjustments, you can define a workflow for your business needs and then activate it with this Manage Workflows for Journal Entry Verification - In General Ledger app. All journal entries and currency adjustments that need to be verified go through the workflow when they meet the preconditions that you define in this app to start a workflow.

Depending on your business requirements, you can determine the approver (e.g. based on HR position) without using the Manage Teams and Responsibilities for General Journal Entry Verification. Please refer to SAP note 3603584 - How to extend Verify General Journal Entries with Custom Rule - SAP for Me on the available extensibility. The step-by-step guide can be found in the attachment of this note.

F3932

Manage Teams and Responsibilities for Journal Entry Verification - In General Ledger

With this feature you can:

  • View the hierarchical structure of teams, sub teams, and super teams for these team categories: FGJEV
Maintain values for custom responsibility definitions in a team

If the requester and approver can be managed using this Fiori app, then it should suffice bearing in mind that it might have some limitations and scalability considerations. The extensibility for custom rule can be considered if necessary.

F6957

Custom Logic

With this app, you can implement BAdIs that have been released by SAP. You can start implementing from the Custom Logic app or from the UI adaptation in an application that can be extended.This is where the extensibility can address the determination of approvers via BAdI.
F4637

Manage Responsibility Rules

With the Manage Responsibility Rules application, you can see a list of standard responsibility rules delivered by SAP application teams. These rules are used to determine agents that are responsible for workflows and situation handling.Familiarise yourself with the concept of CDS views.
F4636

Manage Responsibility Contexts

With the Manage Responsibility Contexts application, you can use the list of standard responsibility contexts delivered by SAP application teams for agent determination.

You can also extend a standard responsibility context by adding your custom agent rule to determine agents that are responsible for your business process tasks, objects, and situation handling.

Use this in conjunction with Manage Responsibility Rules

F1306

Maintain Email Templates

Using this app, you can define email templates to streamline your email correspondence. Templates consist of fixed content (such as subject or disclaimer) and variable parts (such as the form of address).

You can put a link in the email notification so that it can be directed to the My Inbox Fiori app. The URL for your different environments can be maintained as variables.

Consider whether it is really necessary to send email notifications (nobody likes a flooded mailbox) or the in-app Fiori notification will be sufficient.

F1590

Configure Software Packages

With this app, you can configure software packages for transporting extension items.

This is necessary for any development.

F1589

Register Extensions for Transport

With this app you, can register extensions for being transported in your production system.

A transport request is still required but this Fiori Apps is necessary for extensions made via Fiori Apps.

The links provided in this blog was at the time that SAP S/4HANA 2025 is already available. Always review the latest features based on your SAP S/4HANA versions. I hope this blog has helped you together with the other available articles in our community.

entry.title: International Trade Compliance Is a Sales Topic. Here's How to Own It.
entry.content:
You're in a customer meeting. The procurement lead asks: "How does SAP handle sanctions screening in our purchase orders?" Or the CFO wants to know: "Can we automate our Intrastat filings?"

If your honest answer is "I'll get back to you on that" — this post is for you.

There's a learning journey for exactly this

SAP Learning has a free, self-paced journey called Positioning International Trade Management. It's built for sales and presales — not consultants, not implementers. Just people who need to walk into a customer conversation and know what they're talking about.

 

 

It covers the topics that actually come up: product classification, Intrastat declarations, sanctions screening, embargo checks, export and import controls. All of it positioned around business value, not configuration steps.

What changes after you complete it

You stop losing momentum in trade-related deals. You handle the first conversation yourself instead of pulling in a specialist. You connect SAP's capabilities directly to what the customer is worried about — compliance risk, manual workload, regulatory exposure.

That's not a small thing. In industries like chemicals, automotive, or industrial manufacturing, trade compliance is often a real buying trigger. Being the person in the room who can speak to it confidently makes a difference.

Who should take it

If you're an account executive, presales engineer, or partner sales professional working anywhere near S/4HANA — this is worth your time. Especially if you've ever avoided the trade compliance topic because it felt like specialist territory.

It isn't. Not after this.

Pass it on

Forward this to your team. Add it to your enablement list. If you manage presales or sales at an SAP partner, this is exactly the kind of thing that separates a sharp team from an average one.

Already done it? Leave a comment — what clicked for you, and how did you use it with a customer?

entry.title: Enhance Your AI Knowledge in SAP Finance: New Learning Journey Now Available
entry.content:
The future of finance is here, and it's powered by artificial intelligence. Are you ready to lead the charge in AI-driven financial transformation? We're excited to announce the launch of our latest learning journey: Applying Business AI Solutions and Expertise in SAP Finance

Why This Learning Journey Matters

In today's rapidly evolving business landscape, finance professionals who can harness the power of AI aren't just staying competitive; they're becoming indispensable. This intermediate learning journey is designed specifically for the next generation of finance professionals who are ready to take the lead in digital transformation and unlock the full potential of SAP's cutting-edge AI solutions.

What You'll Learn

This learning journey takes you on an immersive exploration of key AI tools that are revolutionizing finance operations:

  • SAP Joule: Discover how this intelligent assistant transforms everyday finance tasks
  • SAP Joule Agents: Learn to leverage specialized AI agents for complex financial processes
  • SAP Joule for Consultants: Master the tools that empower finance consulting excellence
  • SAP Business Data Cloud in Finance: Unlock the potential of intelligent data management

A Hands-On Learning Experience

What sets this learning journey apart is its practical, experience-driven approach. You explore Joule features in Finance firsthand through practical, hands-on exercises that enable learners to experience these capabilities themselves. You'll follow a fictitious consultant's journey as they implement AI tools across various finance business processes, giving you real-world context and actionable insights you can apply immediately.

Starting Your AI Journey? We've Got You Covered

If you're new to AI in finance, we recommend starting with our beginner-level learning journey: Discovering SAP Business AI Capabilities for SAP Finance. This foundational course introduces you to the core concepts and capabilities of AI in the finance domain, providing the perfect stepping stone before diving into the more advanced, hands-on content in our new intermediate learning journey. Whether you're taking your first steps or ready to deepen your expertise, we have a path designed for your learning needs.

Curious?

The intersection of AI and finance represents one of the most exciting opportunities in today's business world. By completing our learning journey, you'll not only gain technical expertise but also position yourself as an expert in the AI-driven future of finance. Start learning right now!
